problem.title: Max Points on a Line
problem.description: Given an array of points where points[i] = [xi, yi] represents a point on the X-Y plane, return the maximum number of points that lie on the same straight line.
assumptions: Use fractions (gcd) to represent slope exactly.

example.explanation: All 3 points are on the line y=x. For each point, count others with same slope.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – HashMap with GCD
solutions.description: For each anchor point, compute the reduced slope (dy/gcd, dx/gcd) to every other point and count how many points share each slope using a HashMap. Using GCD to normalize the slope fraction avoids floating-point precision errors. The key insight is that all points collinear with the anchor share the same normalized slope, so the maximum count plus the anchor itself gives the longest line through that point.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List
from collections import defaultdict
from math import gcd

def max_points(points: List[List[int]]) -> int:
    if len(points) <= 2:
        return len(points)

    max_pts = 0
    for i in range(len(points)):
        slopes: dict = defaultdict(int)
        duplicates = 0
        for j in range(len(points)):
            if i == j:
                continue
            if points[i] == points[j]:
                duplicates += 1
                continue
            dx = points[j][0] - points[i][0]
            dy = points[j][1] - points[i][1]
            g = gcd(abs(dx), abs(dy))
            # Normalize sign so slope is canonical
            if dx < 0:
                dx, dy = -dx, -dy
            slope = (dx // g, dy // g)
            slopes[slope] += 1
        best = max(slopes.values(), default=0)
        max_pts = max(max_pts, best + duplicates + 1)
    return max_pts
